---
title: "East of Eden"
author: "John Steinbeck"
date: 2025-01-15
categories: [Philosophy, Fiction, Classics, Book]
image: cover.jpg
subtitle: ★★★★★
body-class: "project-detail-view"
include-before-body:
  text: "<div style='margin-top: 50px;'></div>"
---

<style>
  #quarto-margin-sidebar {
    padding-top: 50px; /* Match this to your header margin */
  }
</style>

## Summary and the Core Premise of the Book
A multi-generational saga retelling the biblical story of Cain and Abel...

## Why I Read This Book

## Key Takeaways
1.  **xxx:** xxxx
2.  **xxx:** xxx

## Reflection
This book shifted my perspective on agency. In a deterministic world (or in a quantitative model), we assume inputs dictate outputs. Steinbeck argues for the "error term"—the human capacity to choose against the grain.

## Favorite Quotes
> "And now that you don't have to be perfect, you can be good."

---
